citations.title: The Moment Problem (Graduate Texts in Mathematics 277)
citations.locator: Theorem 10.7 (interior of the truncated moment cone)
citations.verbatim_statement: The truncated moment cone of the (Hamburger) moment problem on R has nonempty interior: an interior point of the moment cone is realized by a representing measure, and every sufficiently small perturbation of an interior truncated moment/cumulant sequence remains realizable by a probability measure with the required finite moments. (Companion sources: Akhiezer (1965), The Classical Moment Problem, ch. 1-2, for the truncated Hamburger existence half; Curto & Fialkow (1991), Houston J. Math 17, 603-635, for flat-extension solvability.)

citations.title: Real Algebraic Geometry (Bochnak-Coste-Roy) and Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, Ch. 5 (Basu-Pollack-Roy): cylindrical algebraic decomposition adapted to a finite polynomial family, and the Tarski-Seidenberg projection theorem
citations.authors: Jacek Bochnak, Michel Coste, Marie-Francoise Roy; Saugata Basu, Richard Pollack, Marie-Francoise Roy
citations.locator: BCR (Springer, Ergebnisse 36, 1998) Sec. 2.3 'Decomposition of Semi-algebraic Sets' (pp. 30-34) and Thm 2.2.1 (Tarski-Seidenberg / projection); BPR (Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, 2nd ed.) Ch. 5 'Decomposition of Semi-Algebraic Sets' -- Def. 5.1 (cylindrical decomposition: section/sector cells), Def. 5.5 (P-invariance, 'cad adapted to P'), Thm 5.6 (existence for every finite P), Notation 5.15 (the Elim projection operator: leading coefficients, truncations, principal subresultant coefficients of (R, dR/dX_k) and of pairs), Thm 5.16 (lifting over an Elim-invariant cell), and the projection theorem of Ch. 2.
citations.verbatim_statement: MATHEMATICAL (non-algorithmic) statement of record. BPR Thm 5.6: 'For every finite set P of polynomials in R[X_1,...,X_k], there is a cylindrical decomposition of R^k adapted to P.' Unwinding BPR Def. 5.1 / Def. 5.5 / Notation 5.15 / Thm 5.16, that decomposition is: finitely many nonempty pairwise-disjoint semialgebraic cells covering R^k, cylindrically arranged; each cell over a cell of R^{k-1} is a SECTION (graph of a continuous real root function) or a SECTOR (band between consecutive roots), obtained by RECURSIVE LIFTING of the projection family Elim generated from P (leading coefficients, truncations, principal subresultant coefficients, iterated down the variable order); every polynomial of P and of that generated family has CONSTANT SIGN on every cell; hence every cell DECIDES every sign condition built from P (each cell is contained in, or disjoint from, the condition's solution set, and each P-semialgebraic set is a union of cells). BCR Sec. 2.3 states the same cylindrical sign-invariant decomposition. PROJECTION / QUANTIFIER ELIMINATION: the image of a semialgebraic set under a coordinate projection is semialgebraic (Tarski-Seidenberg; BCR Thm 2.2.1; BPR Ch. 2 Projection Theorem for Semi-Algebraic Sets). NOTHING ABOVE IS AN EFFECTIVITY/COMPUTABILITY CLAIM: BPR Ch. 5 is mathematics (the algorithms live in BPR Ch. 11), and the cited assertion is the EXISTENCE + STRUCTURE of the decomposition, which is all this development consumes. TWO DISCLOSED NORMALIZATIONS, neither a strengthening of content: (i) the printed theorems fix the variable order X_1,...,X_k and eliminate X_k first; the arbitrary-variable-order form used here is the immediate corollary obtained by relabelling coordinates. (ii) BPR's Elim lists principal subresultant coefficients of (R, dR/dX_k) together with lcof(R); the discriminant is sRes_0(R, dR/dX_k) divided by lcof(R) up to sign, so sign-invariance for Elim implies sign-invariance for the discriminant family named here -- adding discriminants (Collins/Arnon-Collins-McCallum phrasing of the same operator) demands nothing beyond BPR's family.

citations.title: Effective rational Groebner elimination and cylindrical algebraic decomposition / quantifier elimination
citations.authors: David A. Cox, John Little, Donal O'Shea; George E. Collins; Saugata Basu, Richard Pollack, Marie-Francoise Roy
citations.locator: Cox-Little-O'Shea, Ideals, Varieties, and Algorithms, 4th ed., Ch. 2 'Groebner Bases' and Ch. 3 'Elimination Theory'; Collins 1975, Quantifier Elimination for Real Closed Fields by Cylindrical Algebraic Decomposition; Basu-Pollack-Roy, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry, 2nd ed., Ch. 11 'Cylindrical Decomposition Algorithm' and Ch. 14 'Quantifier Elimination'; Basu, Algorithms in Real Algebraic Geometry: A Survey (2017), Sec. 2.1.2, Thm. 2.4 for the explicit `(sD)^(2^{O(N)})` CAD complexity form.
citations.verbatim_statement: ALGORITHMIC statement of record, kept distinct from cite:bcr-bpr-cad. Given finite polynomial input over Q (or Q(i)) and a monomial/variable order, Buchberger's terminating algorithm computes a finite Groebner basis; an elimination order computes the corresponding elimination ideals, and standard auxiliary-variable constructions compute saturation and ideal intersection. Given a finite rational real-polynomial family and variable order, the Collins/BPR cylindrical-decomposition algorithm terminates and returns finite exact real-algebraic symbolic data for a sign-invariant recursively section/sector CAD, thereby deciding quantified Boolean combinations of polynomial equalities and inequalities and permitting cellwise witness retention. For s input polynomials of degree at most D in N variables, the CAD/QE cost has the standard doubly-exponential form (sD)^(2^{O(N)}). The formal interface may expose universal natural constants a>=1 and b fixed before every input family and certify operations <= (max(2,sD))^(2^(a*N+b)); it may not set the hidden exponent constant to 1 or select constants after seeing the input. This general statement assumes no LiNGAM map, moment certificate, exceptional locus, fiber equality, atlas label, or paper-specific conclusion.
